architecture.summary: The system augments LLM reasoning for catalyst discovery using Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) over the LLM's generation space. The LLM generates candidate reasoning steps (nodes in the search tree); MCTS explores and evaluates these steps to navigate the combinatorial space of chemical properties and trade-offs. Information flows as: query → LLM generates reasoning steps → MCTS selects/expands/evaluates nodes → aggregates best reasoning path → outputs final answer. The mechanism treats the LLM as a generative engine for step proposals, not as a frozen oracle.

biblio.title: Monte Carlo Thought Search: Large Language Model Querying for Complex Scientific Reasoning in Catalyst Design

claims.dataset: diverse questions written by catalysis researchers for reasoning about novel chemical conversion processes
claims.statement: our approach can improve over the best baseline by 25.8%
our approach can augment scientist's reasoning and discovery process with novel insights
large language models have demonstrated novel capabilities for chemistry through complex instruction following capabilities and high quality reasoning
a goal-driven combinatorial search using LLMs has not been explored in detail
